content.text:
Decision Support Information Systems
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEF31  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30 / TP: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Understand the fundamentals of decision-making and the different approaches
Understand the fundamental concepts of decision support information systems
Study decision support IS (DSIS) and their role in the decision-making process
Develop skills in designing and implementing decision solutions
Acquire knowledge of decision support tools and techniques
Become familiar with data analysis and modelling techniques in a decision-making context
Analyse and evaluate the impact of DSIS on the organisation and its effectiveness
Recommended prior knowledge:
Databases, programming, algorithmics, information systems, statistics and operations research.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Foundations of Decision-Making
Definition of decision, decision-making process
Types of decisions (strategic, tactical, operational)
Decision-making approaches (rational, intuitive, etc.)
Factors influencing decision (information, uncertainty, risk)
Information sources and their relevance
Decision Support Information Systems (DSIS)
Definition and role of DSIS in decision-making
DSIS typology and conceptual framework: data, information, knowledge, wisdom, experience
DSIS architecture: client-server, web, data warehouses, datamarts, etc.
DSIS types: TPS, MIS, DSS, ESS
Data sources and integration: ETL techniques
Dimensional data modelling: star, snowflake
Analysis and reporting tools: dashboards, OLAP queries
Decision Support Tools and Technologies
DBMS: Oracle, MySQL, SQL Server
Business Intelligence tools: SAP BusinessObjects, Tableau, Power BI
Query languages: SQL
Modelling and simulation; linear programming, decision trees
Sensitivity analysis, scenario analysis
Data mining tools; AI and machine learning for decision-making
DSIS Design and Implementation
DSIS development stages: needs analysis, design, development, deployment, maintenance
Project management methodologies: Agile, Waterfall
Data security and confidentiality in DSIS
Organisational and human aspects of DSIS
Decision Databases
Data models for data warehouses (star, snowflake model)
Design and implementation of data warehouses
Query performance optimisation
Data quality and metadata management
DSIS Evaluation and Management
DSIS evaluation criteria (performance, usability, etc.)
DSIS project planning and management
Security and data confidentiality
Future trends and prospects of DSIS
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
Ph. Vallin and D. Vanderpooten (2002).
Aide à la décision : une approche par les cas
. Ellipses, Paris.
Turban, E., Volonino, L., & McLean, E. R. (2018).
Information Technology for Management
. Wiley.
Inmon, W. H. (2005).
Building the Data Warehouse
. John Wiley & Sons.
Kimball, R., & Ross, M. (2013).
The Data Warehouse Toolkit: The Definitive Guide to Dimensional Modeling
. John Wiley & Sons.
